prompt: Build the Meridian Training Analytics Management System, an on-premise training platform combining student and mentor learning tools, operational reporting, and offline-first session capture. The Angular web client serves four roles (Student, Corporate Mentor, Faculty Mentor, Administrator), each with role-appropriate navigation and data scope. Users self-register with username and password and remain pending until an Administrator approves the account within 2 business days; passwords must be at least 12 characters with at least one number and one symbol, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Students run training sessions with an in-session timer, a between-set rest timer defaulting to 60 seconds and adjustable within 15-300 seconds, and per-activity completion check-offs; the UI surfaces immediate 'saved locally' feedback when offline and supports resume-after-interruption via a one-tap 'continue session' flow. Mentors and admins consume learning and assessment analytics covering mastery trends, wrong-answer distributions, weak knowledge-point analysis, and item difficulty/discrimination at learner, cohort, and course levels, filterable by date range, location, instructor, and course version. The Operations & Reporting Center delivers configurable reports on enrollments, seat utilization, refund/return rate, training-material inventory, and certifications expiring within 30/60/90 days, with scheduled CSV/PDF exports to a local path, in-app notifications using editable templates, and permission-isolated scopes so corporate mentors see only their organization's learners and purchases. The Spring Boot backend exposes a decoupled REST API over PostgreSQL (users, roles, courses, assessment items, attempts, session logs, operational transactions, audit events). Offline resilience uses IndexedDB for session logs and draft assessments; on LAN reconnect, background sync reconciles via idempotent request keys and last-write-wins, preventing duplicates. Data governance enforces classification tiers (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted) and masks sensitive fields by default (e.g., last 4 characters of employee IDs, partial contacts) unless explicitly permitted. Sensitive values are AES-256 encrypted at rest, passwords are BCrypt salted+hashed, HTTPS is served with a locally managed certificate, and standard security headers are enforced. End-to-end audit logging captures logins, exports, permission changes, and deletions; anomaly alerts fire in-app for new device fingerprints, access outside allowed IP ranges, or more than 20 export attempts in 10 minutes. APIs apply token-based access, configurable rate limits, and approval workflows for permission changes and data exports. Backup and DR run fully offline: nightly incremental and weekly full PostgreSQL backups to an administrator-defined local path, 30-day default retention, quarterly recovery drills with recorded results, an optional 14-day recycle bin, and a documented failover to a standby server on the same network.
database: PostgreSQL (primary, system-of-record); IndexedDB (secondary, client-side offline cache)
